title: Standalone mini security command
goal: Add a 'mini security' command that opens an interactive review session built by buildSecurityReviewContext over the last phase (default when no range flags are given) or a range (--from-phase/--to-phase, --from/--to), derives the .mini/security/<range>.md output path from the range, and runs with a scoped tool set — mirroring adversarialProject.ts. Open decision for discuss: how the reviewer is permitted to write the .md report (a Write tool scoped to .mini/security/ vs a scoped CLI write like 'mini findings add'); the latter would mean revising the phase-169 prompt. Verify Claude Code permission scoping via Context7 rather than guessing, and confirm the single-last-phase default passes through the genesis fallback for a first phase without preSha.

steps.title: Resolve security target: range input + output path
steps.detail: Helper resolveSecurityTarget(cwd, input) -> { input: RangeInput, outputPath } | null. No flags: pick last 'done' phase from header, return {fromPhase:id,toPhase:id} and .mini/security/phase-<N>.md (error if no done phase). Phase flags -> range-<A>-<B>.md. Ref flags -> resolve each ref to a short SHA -> range-<shortFrom>-<shortTo>.md (error on invalid ref). Genesis fallback stays in resolveRange. Unit tests cover all 3 modes + no-done-phase + invalid ref.

steps.title: mini security command + scoped tool set
steps.detail: src/commands/security.ts mirrors adversarialProject.ts: no-project guard, call resolveSecurityTarget, buildSecurityReviewContext(cwd, input, outputPath), print prompt, ask confirm, run workWithClaude with SECURITY_ALLOWED_TOOLS (Read/Grep/Glob/LS, read-only git diff/log/show, Write(.mini/security/**) instead of mini findings add), status line + hint pointing at the report path. Comment documents that without --permission-mode a write outside the dir prompts, not a hard block.

steps.title: Pinning test for SECURITY_ALLOWED_TOOLS
steps.detail: Standalone test fixing the exact tool set (like adversarial-project) so drift fails independently; asserts Write(.mini/security/**) present and mini findings add / Edit absent.

steps.title: Register mini security in CLI
steps.detail: cli.ts .command('security') with --from-phase/--to-phase/--from/--to (same parsers as adversarial-project), report-only description noting it writes .mini/security/<range>.md, dynamic import dispatching to security({...}).

steps.title: Command behavior test
steps.detail: Following adversarialProject.test.ts (mock ask, workWithClaude, builder): assert security() passes SECURITY_ALLOWED_TOOLS to workWithClaude, builder gets the right outputPath (default -> phase-N.md, phase mode -> range-A-B.md), null builder result starts no session, and confirm:false starts no session.

resolvedVerify: Live Claude permission scoping of Write(.mini/security/**)
